registry.description: Hatred is the inner state of actively opposing the good of another — not mere dislike or indifference but the settled disposition that wishes harm or ruin on a person. The Hebrew vocabulary is stark and simple. Scripture takes hatred seriously as a moral state: hating one's brother is equated in 1 John with murder. God is described as hating — not people as such, but wickedness, injustice, and what destroys his creation. There is also a place in Scripture for what is called holy hatred: the vigorous opposition to what is genuinely evil.

terms.medium_def:
1) hating, hatred, hate
1a) hatred
1a1) of man, God

terms.medium_def:
1) to hate, be hateful
1a) (Qal) to hate
1a1) of man
1a2) of God
1a3) hater, one hating, enemy (participle) (subst)
1b) (Niphal) to be hated
1c) (Piel) hater (participle)
1c1) of persons, nations, God, wisdom
Aramaic equivalent: se.ne (שְׂנָא "to hate" H8131)

terms.medium_def:
A location first mentioned at Deu.3.8; 
referred to as Hermon_Mount or Hermon (חֶרְמוֹן), or Hermon (חֶרְמוֹנִים), or (Mount )Baal-hermon or Baal-hermon (NIV= Baal Hermon) (בַּ֫עַל חֶרְמוֹן), or Baal-hamon (NIV= Baal Hamon) (בַּ֫עַל הָמוֹן), or Baal-gad (NIV= Baal Gad) (בַּ֫עַל גָּד), or Senir (שְׂנִיר), or (Mount )Sirion (KJV= Sion) (שִׂיאֹן), or Sirion (KJV= "field," NIV= "slope") (שָׂדַי), or Sirion (שִׂרְיֹן). § the Amorite name for Mount Hermon
